selection: Use the ordered PARD flaring kit only if it includes a true 4.75 mm / 3/16 in die and makes 45-degree double/inverted flares
exact_spec: Must clamp and flare 4.75 mm / 3/16 in brake tube; double/inverted flare required; practice on scrap before vehicle lines
next_action: Receipt-check the delivered kit before any line fabrication; if no 4.75 mm / 3/16 in die is present buy or borrow a dedicated brake-line flaring tool
reject_if: Only 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16 mm dies are supplied; tool makes only single or ISO bubble flares; clamp slips on 4.75 mm tube
notes: This is the gating tool. Do not assume a 4 mm die is acceptable for 4.75 mm brake tube.

selection: Use the ordered Harden 3-22 mm mini tube cutter if it cuts 4.75 mm tube squarely
exact_spec: Clean square cuts on 4.75 mm / 3/16 in, 6 mm, and 8 mm tube without crushing
next_action: Receipt-check cutter wheel action and test on scrap tube
reject_if: Cutter spirals, crushes, or leaves a severe burr on 4.75 mm tube
notes: A square cut is mandatory for repeatable double/inverted flares.

selection: Buy a compact tube deburrer/reamer unless the delivered flaring kit includes a good one
exact_spec: Must deburr inside and outside of 4.75 mm / 3/16 in brake tube after every cut
next_action: Buy before making lines; keep with cutter/flaring kit
reject_if: No internal deburrer; only a file or knife is available; burrs remain inside tube
notes: Burrs can crack or distort the flare and can contaminate hydraulics.

selection: Buy or borrow a dedicated 4.75 mm / 3/16 in bender if using bundy steel or making tight bends; CuNi can be hand-formed for broad bends
exact_spec: Bender groove must explicitly support 4.75 mm / 3/16 in tube; existing Daraz bender covers 1/4, 5/16, 3/8 only
next_action: Do not use the existing 1/4 in groove for tight 3/16 brake bends unless test bends prove no flattening
reject_if: Bender lacks 4.75 mm / 3/16 groove; tube wrinkles, flats, or kinks
notes: Existing Daraz bender remains useful for larger 1/4, 5/16, and 3/8 tube, not as the primary brake-line bender.

selection: Buy metric flare-nut/line wrenches before loosening old hydraulic fittings
exact_spec: At minimum cover likely brake-line nut hex sizes around 10, 11, 12, 14, and 17 mm; sample-match on vehicle
next_action: Buy a small set or individual 10x12 plus 12x14 and 14x17 sizes before removal
reject_if: Only open-end spanners are available; wrench flexes or rounds old nuts
notes: Use penetrating oil and the correct line wrench before forcing old fittings.

selection: Use calipers and a metric thread pitch gauge to identify flare nuts and port threads
exact_spec: Must distinguish M10x1.0, M10x1.25, M12x1.0, and any non-metric fitted hardware before buying fittings
next_action: Confirm on hand before fitting purchase
reject_if: Threads are guessed from vehicle year or by eye only
notes: Do not buy flare nuts by catalog logic alone because the brake system has prior conversion history.

selection: Prefer new brake-rated CuNi/Cunifer 4.75 mm / 3/16 in tube; fallback is brake-rated bundy steel
exact_spec: 10000 mm minimum if combining brake and clutch; 12000 mm preferred; no bare copper
next_action: Quote/buy tube stock from Longman or a hydraulic brake pipe specialist
reject_if: Bare copper, plumbing tube, used tube, unknown rating, or stainless tube that the shop/tool cannot flare cleanly
notes: CuNi/Cunifer is preferred for in-house shaping because it is easier to bend and flare; bundy steel is acceptable if the fabricator can form clean bends and flares.

selection: Buy new double/inverted flare nuts only after old nut thread and port seat are confirmed
exact_spec: Brake-rated fittings for 4.75 mm / 3/16 in tube; likely Toyota-style metric hardware but sample controls final thread
next_action: Remove one old line intact; photograph straight-on flare face; measure nut thread and seat; then buy extras for practice and final lines
reject_if: Seller offers single flare, ISO bubble, plumbing compression fittings, or cannot match thread/seat
notes: Buy enough extra nuts to allow scrap/practice flares before final lines.

item: Rear axle tee and inline unions
selection: Replace only with brake-rated double/inverted flare hardware matched to old samples
exact_spec: Match thread, flare seat, port orientation, mounting hole/bracket style, and tube entry angles
next_action: Take old tee/union or clear photos to hydraulic specialist before purchase
reject_if: Plumbing brass, compression unions, unknown seat, or wrong port orientation
notes: Replace seized, corroded, damaged, or mismatched tees/unions rather than forcing old fittings into new lines.

selection: Buy complete crimped brake flex hose assemblies by sample
exact_spec: Front left, front right, and rear center; DOT/SAE J1401 or OEM-equivalent; copy free length, bracket groove, and ends
next_action: Remove or measure old hoses and fitted ends before payment
reject_if: Generic roll hose, reused hose, unmatched ends, or no brake hydraulic rating
notes: Do not fabricate flex hoses locally unless the shop is a certified hydraulic brake hose assembler and can match the old fittings.

selection: Buy rubber-lined P-clips, brake hose U-clips, axle line supports, grommets, edge trim, and plated fasteners
exact_spec: 4.75 mm P-clips plus mixed support hardware; support hard lines every 300-400 mm and near unions/bends
reject_if: Cable ties, bare metal clips, loose U-clips, or unsupported spans
notes: Support quality is part of brake-line reliability, not cosmetic hardware.

selection: Buy remaining caps/plugs, brake cleaner, catch bottle or bleeder kit, rags, and catch tray before opening lines
exact_spec: DOT 3 fluid, clear bleed hose, and nitrile gloves are already received; remaining consumables must be on hand before disassembly
next_action: Buy before the first hydraulic fitting is opened
reject_if: No line caps/plugs; no catch bottle/tray; brake cleaner unavailable
notes: Open ports should be capped immediately to keep dirt and moisture out.

selection: Reserve extra tube length and spare flare nuts for bench practice
exact_spec: Make at least 5 practice double/inverted flares on the selected tube before fabricating vehicle lines
next_action: Practice cut/deburr/flare sequence and inspect flare symmetry before cutting final lines
reject_if: First flare attempted on final vehicle line; flare is off-center, cracked, folded unevenly, or nut was forgotten
notes: Practice confirms tool setup, die depth, deburring, and nut orientation.
